text: Vitamin D deficiency increases the risk of injury in athletes.
Vitamin D supplementation significantly improves aerobic capacity measures including maximal oxygen uptake and physical working capacity in elite athletes compared to placebo.
Vitamin D supplementation consistently increases anaerobic power and strength in elite athletes compared to non-supplemented groups.
specific_value: 5 of 7 studies showed increased anaerobic power and strength

text: Vitamin D supplementation does not consistently improve sprint speed in elite athletes.
specific_value: 4 of 6 studies found no significant difference

text: Vitamin D supplementation does not consistently reduce inflammatory cytokine levels in elite athletes, with only isolated findings of lower interleukin-6 levels.
Athletes with lower serum vitamin D levels show higher serum biomarkers of bone turnover including bone-specific alkaline phosphatase, parathyroid hormone, and N-terminal telopeptide.
The effect of vitamin D supplementation on bone mineral density in athletes is inconclusive, with conflicting results across studies.
